# Private-content denylist — patterns that must never reach an arqtiqa/* repo.
# Per AQS-7 / private-content-firewall decision (2026-05-05).
#
# Format: one POSIX extended regex per line. Lines starting with `#` and blank
# lines are ignored. No inline comments — put commentary on its own line.
#
# Both `scripts/check-private-content.sh` (CI / pre-commit hook gate) and a
# future GitHub Actions workflow read this file. Add patterns as new leak
# vectors are discovered; remove only after confirming the matched content
# was a false positive.
#
# Refining a noisy pattern: prefer making it more specific over removing it.
# E.g. add `\b` word boundaries, anchor the email at `@`, or scope to the
# leak position (gitconfig `email = ` line vs free text).

# ---- Personal identity (this operator) ----

# Real name — case-insensitive.
(?i)\b(mariusz|wiercinski)\b

# Personal email — exact.
mw4dev@gmail\.com

# ---- Real org / venture domains for this operator ----
# Ventures the operator is affiliated with; surfacing them in arqtiqa/*
# leaks org affiliation. Match the @ to keep noise low.
@(k11\.vc|kinguin\.net|viktorsway\.com|sylviaworld\.com)\b

# ---- Machine identifiers ----

# Mac hostnames following the operator's naming scheme: MW-(Mac Studio /
# MacBook / Mac Mini)-<chip><suffix>. Generic "MacBook" brand mentions
# are intentionally not flagged — the MW- prefix above catches real
# hostnames; flagging "MacBook" alone gave too many false positives in
# discussion / spec text.
\bMW-(MS|MB|MM)-[A-Z0-9-]+\b

# ---- Private network ranges ----
# Catches literal IPs from RFC1918 home/lan ranges. Uses anchored octets
# to avoid matching version numbers like "10.0.1" in random text.
\b(192\.168\.|10\.0\.|10\.1\.|172\.(1[6-9]|2[0-9]|3[0-1])\.)[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\b

# ---- Tokens / secrets ----
# Never committed regardless of which repo. Pattern shapes from the issuing
# service's documented format.

# GitHub personal access tokens (classic + fine-grained).
ghp_[A-Za-z0-9]{36,}
gho_[A-Za-z0-9]{36,}
github_pat_[A-Za-z0-9_]{82,}

# Linear API tokens.
lin_api_[A-Za-z0-9]{40,}

# 1Password literal vault/item references that pin a real path.
op://[A-Za-z0-9_-]+/[A-Za-z0-9_-]+
